title: try_send over send in send_event: prevents blocking async executor on full channel
description: send_event uses std::sync::mpsc::SyncSender::try_send (non-blocking) rather than send (blocking). If the UI thread falls behind and the 256-slot channel fills, a blocking send would stall the tokio task driving all JMAP I/O — effectively freezing network operations. try_send drops the event instead, which is safe because the events dropped under backpressure (transient status updates, non-critical errors) can be re-derived from the next event or are genuinely transient. A future contributor might change this to send 'to avoid dropping events', not realising they would be blocking an async executor thread and potentially deadlocking or starving the runtime.
close_reason: Already addressed: send_event uses unbounded channel; send() is always non-blocking and cannot block the async executor
